Title: Vehicle Registration using ThaID/NDID
KeyTerms: Government e-services, Public Service Delivery
Description: Thailand uses ThaID and National Digital ID (NDID) for vehicle registration. Individuals authenticate their identity through facial verification using the ThaID mobile application or NDID federated login. The system securely confirms the user’s identity and routes the authentication result to the Department of Land Transport (DLT) portal. The portal then automatically retrieves the individual’s verified personal data. This enables individuals to avail of services, such as annual vehicle re-registration, vehicle tax payment, initiation of an ownership transfer, and retrieval of registration certificate status, entirely online.

Title: Single Sign On (SSO) using ThaID and NDID
KeyTerms: Single sign-on, Government e-services, Public Service Delivery
Description: Thailand’s digital government ecosystem integrates ThaID and National Digital ID (NDID) for secure single sign-on access across multiple government portals. Residents authenticate using facial verification through the ThaID mobile application or NDID federated login through licensed identity providers. After successful authentication, the system securely routes the confirmation to the relying government portal. This enables the user to access services, such as digital approvals, complaint submission, court services, and regulatory filings.

Title: Tax Filing using NDID/ThaID
KeyTerms: Income Tax, Tax filing
Description: The Revenue Department in Thailand supports secure online tax filing and taxpayer authentication using National Digital ID (NDID) or ThaID credentials. Individuals log in to the e-filing portal using their 13-digit personal identification number and OTP- or NDID-based authentication. For NDID-based verification, the user approves the authentication request through their chosen identity provider, such as ThaID or a participating bank app. Upon successful identity verification, individuals file tax returns. This integration of NDID with tax systems improves efficiency across the e-filing system.

Title: Bank Account Opening using NDID/ThaID
KeyTerms: NDID, ThaID, eKYC, Banking, Insurance, Customer onboarding, Digital Onboarding, Paperless Banking
Description: Thailand’s National Digital ID (NDID) platform enables in-person or remote e-KYC processes across banking, insurance, and lending sectors. Customers initiate onboarding through a bank or financial institution’s application and choose an identity provider, such as ThaID or a licensed bank app. Authentication is completed using multi-factor methods, including OTP or PIN and face authentication. Identity is verified against the Department of Provincial Administration (DOPA) registry. On successful verification, verified details are shared with banks and insurance companies.

Title: Sim Registration (eKYC) via NDID/ThaID
KeyTerms: KYC, Electronic Know Your Customer (eKYC), SIM registration, Customer Onboarding, Digital Onboarding
Description: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) mandates that customers present a valid identification (ID) when they purchase and register SIM cards in Thailand. During onboarding, service providers verify the customer’s identity information against the central registry, which is maintained by the Department of Provincial Administration (DOPA). This verification is conducted through National Digital ID (NDID) or ThaID using biometric authentication. Upon successful verification, the provider completes the SIM registration process.

Title: e-Government Services using Singpass
KeyTerms: Single sign-on, Government e-services, Public Service Delivery
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Singpass is Singapore’s national digital identity and serves as the primary login mechanism for government digital services. Singpass users can select “Log in with Singpass” on a government website or mobile application and complete authentication through the Singpass app, with a 6‑digit passcode, device biometrics, or an OTP as part of multi‑factor authentication. It also acts as a single sign-on, which allows users to access various services across government agencies. In-person service counters also accept Singpass-based verification for faster service delivery, which reduces reliance on physical cards and paper forms.

Title: Workfare Income Supplement (Access to Government Disbursement Benefits using Singpass)
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Social Security, Government Disbursement
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Launched in 2007, the Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) program provides cash payments and CPF top-ups to boost the income and retirement savings of lower-wage workers. Beneficiaries must authenticate with Singpass to access WIS e-services, such as to check eligibility, view payment amounts, and update bank account details for disbursement. Singpass is also used to log in for special payouts, such as the Workfare Special Payment (WSP).

Title: Silver Support Scheme (Access to Government Disbursement Benefits using Singpass)
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Social Security, Government Disbursement
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Singpass serves as the authentication method to check the eligibility for, or update the details under, the Silver Support Scheme. Administered by the Ministry of Manpower, the scheme provides quarterly cash support to low-income seniors. Eligibility assessment and payment disbursement are automated through the Central Provident Fund (CPF) digital services. Nearly 290,000 Singapore Citizens aged 65 years and above currently benefit from this scheme.

Title: Healthcare access via HealthHub using Singpass authentication
KeyTerms: HealthHub, Health records, Appointments, Vaccination records
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Singpass is the standard authentication method to access HealthHub, Singapore’s national digital healthcare platform for personal health services. Users authenticate through Singpass to view and manage sensitive health information, such as health records, vaccinations, test results, and medication-related details, as well as to book or manage appointments. Singpass users can book an appointment remotely or at clinics or hospitals. Since HealthHub stores sensitive personal health data, 2FA has been mandatory for accessing it since 2017.

Title: e-Stamping using Singpass
KeyTerms: e-Stamping, Property Transactions, Housing Serivces
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Singpass authenticates users who access housing and property-related e-services, such as common transactions linked to public housing and property management. Users can submit documents for stamping, retrieve stamp certificates, and manage stamping records through Singpass authentication.

Title: e-Sign using Singpass
KeyTerms: e-Sign, Digital Signature, Secure Electronic Signature
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: The Sign with Singpass platform allows individuals to digitally sign documents. Built on the Singpass ecosystem, it enables users to sign contracts and other agreements remotely through the Singpass app. Signatures created through Sign with Singpass are legally recognized as secure electronic signatures under Singapore’s Electronic Transactions Act 2010. They are legally binding and accepted by both government agencies and private organizations. Parties can sign documents instantly and securely without the need to be in the same place or sign physical papers, which makes transactions faster and more convenient.

Title: e-KYC for SIM/e-SIM verification using Singpass
KeyTerms: KYC, Electronic Know Your Customers (eKYC), SIM registration, Customer Onboarding, Digital Onboarding
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description: Singpass users can self-register for a SIM card in Singapore. Users can conveniently activate the SIM/e-SIM from anywhere, anytime by authenticating through Singpass. The customer initiates a SIM registration activation request through the telecom service provider's app or website and verifies their identity through Singpass. This process ties the activation to the correct person and reduces risks, such as identity impersonation and fraudulent activations. It also improves customer experience by allowing fast self-service activation while it maintains compliance with identity requirements for mobile services.

Title: Tax Filing using Singpass
KeyTerms: Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS), Income Tax, Tax filing
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description:
The Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS) operates the Singapore tax portal. The portal offers services to taxpayers, such as submission of income tax returns, requests for extensions of time to file, appeals for waivers of late filing fees or payment penalties, and access to official letters and notices. Taxpayers authenticate themselves through Singpass to access the portal. After successful authentication, taxpayers can submit returns, review tax assessments, manage payments, and receive official communication. 
The MyInfo feature enables taxpayers to pre-fill their details from verified government sources, which reduces errors and simplifies the process. As these services involve sensitive personal and financial information, strong authentication mechanisms are needed to prevent unauthorized access. Two-factor authentication (2FA) through Singpass is therefore mandatory to file taxes and access other services on the IRAS tax portal.

Title: Consolidated financial data access via Singapore Financial Data Exchange (SGFinDex) using Singpass authentication
KeyTerms: SGFinDex, Financial Data Exchange, Open Banking, Financial Planning, Data Consolidation, Consolidated View, Wealth Management
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description:
Singapore Financial Data Exchange (SGFinDex) is a world-first national platform that uses the National Digital Identity (NDI). It enables individuals to retrieve and view their financial information from multiple sources, such as banks, insurers, and selected government agencies, within a single participating financial application. 
Singpass serves as the primary authentication and consent mechanism, which enables users to authorize the retrieval of data from various institutions. Users can consolidate their financial information into a single view instead of logging into each provider separately. These data include account balances, loans, insurance policies, and relevant government-held information, such as CPF or tax records. 
The platform prioritizes user control. The user initiates retrieval on demand and explicitly authorizes data access. SGFinDex itself does not store data; it merely acts as the transmission rail between institutions. This consolidation allows users to view their savings, investments, loans, and insurance coverage in a single dashboard, which facilitates informed planning and cash flow management. 
As of late 2024, SGFinDex has supported more than 150,000 users and facilitated more than 620,000 data retrievals, which reflects a high level of trust and utility in the consolidated data framework.

Title: Bank account opening using Singpass and MyInfo
KeyTerms: Finance, Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer, MyInfo, Customer onboarding, Digital Onboarding, Paperless Banking
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multifactor authentication (OTP and Singpass Face Verification (SFV))
Description:
Banks in Singapore use Singpass as a trusted authentication method for digital onboarding and account opening. A customer starts account opening through the bank’s app or website, authenticates with Singpass, and then consents to share verified identity attributes through MyInfo.
This service enables customers to auto-populate all the necessary details. As a result, there is no need to manually enter personal details or upload identity documents scans, since key attributes, such as name, address, date of birth, and government-verified identifiers are retrieved directly from authoritative sources. 
When higher assurance is required, users can strengthen identity proofing during remote onboarding through Singpass Face Verification as an additional step. This helps reduce the risks of impersonation or synthetic identity fraud. 
For banks, the workflow improves data accuracy and enables faster decision-making, while customers benefit from fewer forms and shorter wait times. Overall, Singpass plus MyInfo establishes a scalable “digital KYC utility” that supports safe and convenient financial onboarding.

Title: Modern Ethiopia Service for Organised Benefits (MESOB) - An integrated digital government services platform
KeyTerms: Public Services, Digital Government, One-Stop-Shop, Service Delivery, Government Efficiency, Citizen Access, MESOB
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - OTP-based authentication (primary), QR code-based offline verification for offline Fayda Auth Service access situations
Description:
In April 2025, the Modern Ethiopia Service for Organized Benefits (MESOB) launched as an integrated one-stop shop for multiple government services. The pilot involved 12 federal government ministries that offered 41 public services in a paperless, fully digital format at the MESOB center. During the pilot’s initial months, more than 12,000 users availed services at MESOB with a 90% satisfaction rate. In June 2025, Deputy Prime Minister Temesgen Tiruneh announced the nationwide rollout of MESOB. 
MESOB functions as a single-stop public service delivery platform that enables citizens with Fayda digital IDs to access a wide range of services and reduces the need for them to physically visit institutions. Citizens can access services through the web portal, mobile application, or physical MESOB service centers for human assistance. 
Currently, the program uses OTP-based authentication and QR code-based offline verification when the Fayda Auth Service is inaccessible. Biometric authentication through face and fingerprint is planned for future implementation.

Title: Digital Farmer Registry for Agricultural Support and Financial Inclusion
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Digital Identity, Financial Inclusion, Farmer Registry, Extension Services, Microfinance Access, Agricultural Transformation
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1 - OTP-based online authentication, QR code-based offline verification at registration points for farmer onboarding
Description:
The Farmer Profile Project by the Ministry of Agriculture in Ethiopia runs in collaboration with the Agricultural Transformation Institute (ATI). It uses the Fayda digital ID to create a comprehensive registry of farmer profiles. 
The project addresses the critical barrier of inaccessible, non-interoperable farmer data, which hampers digital service delivery. It involves data collection, validation, integration, and enhancement of existing farmer-centric databases. It also ensures interoperability with other systems.

Title: Digital ID for refugees
KeyTerms: Refugee
Description:
The National ID Program (NIDP), the UNHCR, and the Refugee and Returnee Service (RRS) signed a data-sharing agreement in January 2024. Since then, efforts have been made to enroll more than 1 million refugees in the Fayda ID system. The goal is to provide refugees with access to healthcare, education, and telecommunications, among others. 
Under this agreement, the PRIMES Interoperability Gateway (PING) is used to exchange data between national authorities and UNHCR to issue identity cards to refugees and asylum-seekers. 
By the end of 2026, Ethiopia plans to issue about 90,000 Fayda digital ID numbers to refugees and returnees.

Title: Digital ID for students
KeyTerms: Education
Description: Ethiopia’s National ID Program and Ministry of Education have adopted the Fayda digital ID as the official student ID across educational institutions. Student biometric data is captured and migrated into the National ID Program's system for Fayda ID generation. The Fayda ID serves as the primary identity credential across academic platforms and streamlines admissions, record management, and national exams.

Title: Bank account opening and mandatory account linkage
KeyTerms: Fayda ID, KYC, Financial Inclusion
Description: The National Bank of Ethiopia and the National ID program jointly announced in November 2023 a mandatory digital ID integration initiative for the banking sector. Its implementation followed a phased approach: A pilot in Addis Ababa commenced on 1st January 2025. The initiative expanded to major cities from 1st July 2025, while the nationwide rollout was completed by 1st January 2026. All bank account holders in Ethiopia are required to link their accounts to Fayda IDs by December 31, 2026. This initiative uses the Fayda digital ID for electronic know-your-customer (e-KYC) processes to enable reliable, real-time identity verification during customer onboarding. Time-and-motion studies demonstrate significant efficiency gains: Banks report 17-63% time savings, with one institution reducing account opening from 43 minutes to nine minutes using Fayda-based e-KYC.

Title: Tax ID (TIN) registration
KeyTerms: Tax, Finance, Income Tax
Description: Digital ID can be used to improve tax administration. In December 2023, Ethiopia began the integration of taxpayer registration with Fayda ID to curb tax evasion and fraud in tax-filing and reduce revenue loss. Since then, each new taxpayer identification number (TIN) is mandatorily linked to the Fayda ID number. In the first six months, the Ministry of Revenue issued 128,000 new TINs through the Fayda ID.

Title: Fayda ID Banking Integration: Ethiopia's Mandatory Digital ID Linking for Financial Inclusion
KeyTerms: Finance, Digital Identity, Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer, Biometric Authentication, National Banking Policy
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Biometric authentication (fingerprint/facial recognition) for e-KYC and account linking verification
Description:
The National Bank of Ethiopia and the National ID Program (NIDP) jointly announced a mandatory digital ID integration initiative for banking in November 2023. The rollout followed a phased plan. The pilot began in Addis Ababa on 1st January 2025, followed by expansion to major cities on 1st July 2025, and a nationwide rollout on 1st January 2026. As per the plan, all bank account holders must link accounts to Fayda IDs by 31st December 2026. 
The initiative uses Fayda, Ethiopia’s national digital ID, for electronic know your customer (e-KYC) processes and enables reliable real-time identity verification during customer onboarding, the launch of digital products, and financial risk control. 
A time-and-motion study revealed significant efficiency gains. Banks reported time savings of 17% to 63%. Notably, an institution even reduced account opening from 43 minutes to 9 minutes through Fayda-based e-KYC. 
More than 11 million Ethiopians have registered for Fayda since its launch. Integration with public key infrastructure (PKI) improves the security of digital transactions and signatures. It modernizes the financial sector, enhances security, reduces fraud, improves customer verification, strengthens data protection, and drives financial inclusion across Ethiopia’s 120-million-strong population.

Title: Social safety net (cash transfer) program
KeyTerms: Social security, Social Protection, Conditional cash transfers
Description: Digital ID enables beneficiary verification in Tanzania's flagship social safety net program. The Productive Social Safety Net (PSSN), operational since 2012 and managed by the Tanzania Social Action Fund (TASAF) under the Ministry of Finance, uses NIN to verify beneficiaries who receive cash transfers and other social assistance. TASAF verifies beneficiary identities through the national identification number (NIN) against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database to ensure transfers reach legitimate recipients and prevent fraud or duplicate enrollments. As of early 2025, PSSN has benefited around 1.37 million households, which cover about 5.2 million individuals. Each household receives between 35,000 and 43,000 TZS (~USD 15-19) per payment cycle. The program's second phase has a total allocation of approximately USD 883.3 million, funded by the World Bank/IDA, government contributions, and multiple development partners.

Title: Social security schemes registration
KeyTerms: Social Security, Social Protection, Pension
Description:
Digital ID verifies and authenticates individuals who register for social security programs and seek to access benefits. Tanzania operates multiple contributory social security programs under the Social Security Regulatory Authority (SSRA), which uses the national identification number (NIN) to verify members against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database. 
The National Social Security Fund (NSSF) provides social security coverage to private-sector workers, NGOs, religious organizations, international organizations, and self-employed persons, with employers and employees each contributing 10% of their monthly salaries. 
As of June 2023, NSSF had approximately 1,189,222 registered members. The Public Service Social Security Fund (PSSSF), operational since 2018, serves public service employees across mainland Tanzania with contributions from both employers and employees. 
The National Informal Sector Social Security Program (NISS), launched in 2021, extends social insurance coverage to informal-sector workers with flexible payment methods that require a minimum monthly contribution of 30,000 TZS (approximately USD 11.4). 
NISS beneficiaries use their NIN to log in to online platforms and mobile apps to view contributions, update personal information, submit claims, and access services. NIN verification ensures accurate member identification, prevents duplicate registrations, streamlines contributions tracking, facilitates benefits disbursement, and enables members to access their social security accounts across different programs throughout their working lives.

Title: Driver's license issuance and motor vehicle registration
KeyTerms: Transport, Vehicle registration, Identity verification
Description: Digital ID verifies individuals who apply for driver's licenses and register motor vehicles. The Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) operates the Integrated Domestic Revenue Administration System (IDRAS), which includes computerized driver's license and motor vehicle registration systems. During driver's license application through the TRA Taxpayer Portal, applicants must provide their national identification number (NIN) for identity verification against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database. For motor vehicle registration, the central motor vehicle registration system requires vehicle owners to provide their NIN as part of the ownership details when they complete registration, re-registration, change of ownership, or other vehicle-related transactions.

Title: ChanjoCovid (COVID-19 vaccination) application
KeyTerms: Health services, Vaccination management, Digital health
Description:
Digital ID verifies citizens' identities when they access Coronavirus (COVID-19) vaccination services through the ChanjoCovid portal developed by the Tanzania Ministry of Health. The national identification number (NIN) is one of the accepted identification documents to book vaccination appointments and access vaccination certificates on the platform. 
The ChanjoCovid system, a COVID-19-specific instance hosted on Tanzania's District Health Information System (DHIS2), support data collection, reporting, and monitoring of vaccination services nationwide. Citizens can use their NIN to register for vaccination, book appointments at vaccination centers, and retrieve their electronic vaccination certificates that comply with international requirements. 
By April 2023, around 38.9 million vaccine doses had been distributed and administered through the ChanjoCovid application, which achieve 51% vaccination coverage of the total population, a dramatic increase from 2.8% in January 2022. 
The NIN integration ensures accurate beneficiary tracking, prevents duplicate registrations, enables effective vaccine delivery monitoring, and supports the generation of verifiable digital vaccination certificates for domestic and international travel purposes.

Title: National Health Insurance Fund (NHIF) registration
KeyTerms: Health insurance, Social protection
Description:
Digital ID verifies citizens' identities during National Health Insurance Fund (NHIF) registration and service access. A national identification number (NIN) is required for NHIF registration, whether completed online through the NHIF self-service portal or in person at an NHIF office. 
During registration, members must provide their NIN and personal details, such as name, email address, and cell phone number. The system verifies the NIN against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database to accurately validate identity and prevent fraudulent enrollments. 
As of June 2023, NHIF had around 1,368,314 contributing members with about 5 million beneficiaries. NIN integration helps NHIF maintain accurate member records, prevent duplicate registrations, streamline claims processing, and ensure health insurance coverage reaches legitimate beneficiaries across all membership categories.

Title: Passport application
KeyTerms: Travel document, Identity verification
Description:
Digital ID facilitates identity verification when Tanzania issues passports. The Tanzania Immigration Department verifies applicants' identities by matching their national identification numbers (NINs) against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database during passport applications. Since January 2018, Tanzania has issued biometric East African e-passports that comply with International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) international standards. 
During a passport application, whether online through the immigration portal or in person at a regional immigration office, applicants must provide their NIN for identity verification. The process includes complete biometric capture, with fingerprints and facial images recorded in the immigration database during in-person appointments at regional offices, Immigration Headquarters, or Tanzanian embassies abroad. 
NIN integration streamlines passport issuance, prevents fraudulent applications, ensures accurate identity verification, and links passport records to the national identity database, which enhances border security and immigration management.

Title: Registration for Tax Identification Number (TIN)
KeyTerms: Tax registration, e-KYC, Tax compliance
Description:
Digital ID enables real-time identity validation during tax identification number (TIN) registration through the Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) Taxpayer Portal. When taxpayers apply for a TIN online, the system authenticates their national identification number (NIN) against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database to verify identity. 
The portal offers two registration pathways: with NIN (for Tanzanian citizens and residents) or without NIN (for foreign nationals who use a passport, driver’s license, voter's ID, or Zanzibar ID). For those with NIN, the system first checks registration status to prevent duplicate TINs, as each individual can have only one TIN. 
The portal provides 24/7 self-service TIN registration, which eliminates the need for physical office visits in most cases. The NIN-linked system streamlines tax administration, improves compliance tracking, and ensures accurate taxpayer identification across all tax-related activities, such as value-added tax (VAT) registration, income tax filing, and business licensing.

Title: Business registrations and licensing
KeyTerms: Business registration, Company incorporation, e-KYC
Description:
Digital ID verifies identity during business registrations and company incorporations through the Online Registration System (ORS) of the Business Registrations and Licensing Agency (BRELA). The national identification number (NIN) is mandatory for all users to create an ORS account and access business registration services. Shareholders, directors, and business owners must provide their NIN during the registration process for their companies, business names, trademarks, and patents. 
For Tanzanian nationals and residents, NIN serves as the primary identity verification mechanism. Directors also require a tax identification number (TIN). The ORS system, launched in January 2018, has digitized all business registration and post-registration services, such as annual returns filing, change notifications for companies, and business license applications. NIN verification against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database ensures accurate identity authentication and helps prevent fraudulent business registrations.

Title: Scholarships and education loan application
KeyTerms: Education, Student verification, e-KYC
Description: Digital ID verifies students' identities during loan and scholarship applications. As of May 2025, the Higher Education Students' Loans Board (HESLB) has made the national identification number (NIN) mandatory for all students who apply for education loans. Students must provide their NIN during the application process. The  National Identification Authority (NIDA) database then verifies the NIN to ensure accurate identity verification and prevent fraud. In the 2024-2025 academic year, around 245,799 students were expected to receive HESLB loans. NIN verification helps ensure loans reach legitimate beneficiaries and improves accountability in education financing.

Title: Bank account opening
KeyTerms: Financial services, e-KYC, AML/CFT
Description: Digital ID enables electronic know your customer (e-KYC) to validate identity before customers open bank accounts and access financial services. Under Bank of Tanzania (BOT) KYC and Anti-Money Laundering/Combating the Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT) regulations, banks and microfinance institutions must verify customer identity during account opening. The national identification number (NIN) serves as the primary proof of identity. Financial institutions verify customers through fingerprint authentication against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database. As of 2023, more than 55 financial institutions had integrated with the National ID System, which enabled more than 16 million people to access financial services through their NIN.

Title: SIM card registration
KeyTerms: Telecom, e-KYC
Description: Digital ID enables electronic know your customer (e-KYC) verification to validate identity before issuing a SIM card. In Tanzania, a national identification number (NIN) verifies a customer's identity. Telecom companies can verify fingerprints against the National Identification Authority (NIDA) database at their service centers. As of 2023, NIDA has registered 53 million out of 54 million SIM card subscribers through NIN.

Title: Bank Account Opening using PhilSys
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer, Digital onboarding
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: Banks in the Philippines use Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) as official proof of identity for customer onboarding and account opening. Customers enter their details, such as name and date of birth, and complete a facial liveness verification through the bank’s mobile app or website. The bank then connects to the National ID eVerify platform to verify the customer’s identity against the PhilSys Registry. After successful identity verification, the platform shares the verified details with the bank. This process eliminates manual document checks, which in turn reduces processing time and cost.

Title: e-Wallet Onboarding using PhilSys
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer, Digital onboarding
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: Leading e-wallets, such as GCash and HelloMoney, utilize the Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) authentication for account creation and verification. Customers enter their details, such as name and date of birth, and complete a facial liveness check through the e-wallet mobile app. An e-wallet account is created upon successful identity verification. This enables fast, remote financial access for millions, such as those in remote areas.

Title: Healthcare access via PhilSys
KeyTerms: Health records, Vaccination records, Health Centre
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: In the Philippines, residents use their Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) credentials to access their PhilHealth membership details and track claims history online. They can also enroll with various accredited health providers across the country with their national ID for verification. During a PhilHealth transaction, the PhilSys verification platform authenticates credentials in real time to confirm the ID's validity. This immediate check validates the identification, prevents fraud, and makes the process both secure and efficient for the member.

Title: Civil Registry Applications using PhilSys
KeyTerms: Government e-services, Public Service Delivery
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: The Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) number enables the application of civil registry documents, such as certificates of live birth, no marriage, and death. The PhilSys registry verifies the identity of each holder against its records using biometric authentication through fingerprint scans or face verification for real-time matching. Successful authentication approves the application.

Title: Food Safety Program using PhilSys
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Social Security, Government Disbursement
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: The Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) number and the electronic Philippine Identification (e-PhilID) verify the identity of beneficiaries in the country. The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), under the zero-hunger food voucher program, distributes pre-loaded cards to enrolled households for food purchases. This approach supports targeted assistance delivery and ensures that benefits reach verified recipients.

Title: Conditional Cash Transfer using PhilSys
KeyTerms: Social Benefit, Social Security, Conditional Cash Transfer
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Biometric authentication (face verification) for e-KYC
Description: The Philippine Identification System (PhilSys) number and the electronic Philippine Identification (e-PhilID) card verify the identity of beneficiaries in the country. The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), under the national poverty reduction strategy and the conditional cash transfer (CCT) program, provides CCTs to poor households. Beneficiaries are authenticated through fingerprints and facial recognition during family development sessions (FDS).

Title: SIM card registration
KeyTerms: Telecom, eKYC
Description: Digital ID verifies customer identities during SIM card registration and activation through the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) verification service. The NIN-SIM linkage has been mandatory for all mobile subscribers in Nigeria since 2020, which includes legal resident foreign nationals. Subscribers submit their national identification number (NIN) to their mobile network operator, which then validates it against the NIMC National Identity Database (NIDB). Subscribers can complete the linkage through multiple channels, such as USSD code (*996#), mobile apps, or in-person visits to network operator service centers. Each subscriber is permitted a maximum of four SIM cards per network operator, all of which must be linked to the same NIN. As of May 2025, approximately 172.67 million SIM cards have been successfully linked to NINs.

Title: Bank account opening
KeyTerms: Financial services, eKYC, AML/CFT
Description: Digital ID verifies customer identities during bank account opening. The national identification number (NIN) stands as a primary identity verification instrument in financial customer onboarding backed by the Mandatory Use of the National Identification Number Regulations 2017. The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) circular of December 2023 mandates all financial institutions to integrate NIN and Bank Verification Number (BVN) into their know your customer (KYC) processes. These institutions include commercial, merchant, non-interest, and payment service banks, other financial institutions (OFIs), and mobile money operators. Under the tiered KYC framework, Tier 1 accounts require either BVN or NIN, while Tier 2 and Tier 3 accounts require both BVN and NIN. The NIN-based verification framework strengthens KYC and AML/CFT compliance, supports financial system stability, reduces identity fraud, and enables accurate customer profiling across Nigeria's banking and FinTech ecosystem.

Title: Taxpayer registration and tax compliance
KeyTerms: Tax registration, TIN, Revenue collection, eKYC
Description: Digital ID verifies taxpayer identities during tax identification number (TIN) registration and ongoing tax compliance processes. In Nigeria, the national identification number (NIN) is one of the accepted identity verification instruments, alongside the Bank Verification Number (BVN) for TIN registration. Individuals can apply for a TIN online with their NIN or BVN. NIN is not explicitly mandatory for TIN issuance but serves as an important alternative to BVN, especially for individuals without bank accounts. The NIN-TIN linkage is part of Nigeria’s broader effort to harmonize taxpayer data across federal and state revenue authorities, connect banking and tax databases, and expand the tax net to capture informal sector participants. As of 2021, Nigeria had approximately 41 million registered taxpayers, though the exact proportion with NIN-linked TINs remains unclear.

Title: Student ID card
KeyTerms: Education, Scholarship, Loan
Description: Digital ID verifies student identities and facilitates access to education loans through a national identification number (NIN)-linked biometric Government-to-People (G2P) cards. In March 2025, NIMC (National Identity Management Commission) and the Nigeria Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) to roll out multi-purpose biometric student ID cards linked to the NIN. The cards feature multiple wallet capabilities, usable online and offline, with anti-counterfeiting measures that NIMC’s Director-General describes as impossible to forge or fake. Beyond loan disbursement, the biometric cards also enable loan tracking to ensure funds are used for their intended educational purposes and promote transparency and accountability in government-to-student transfers. NELFUND launched its loan portal in May 2024 and has since supported more than 437,000 students. NELFUND sought to register 1.2 million students by the end of 2025 through the NIN-linked student ID card.

Title: Employees compensation scheme
KeyTerms: Social insurance, Workforce verification, eKYC
Description: Digital ID is being integrated to verify worker identities and streamline compensation claims under Nigeria’s Employee Compensation Scheme (ECS). The Nigeria Social Insurance Trust Fund (NSITF) and the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) signed an agreement in May 2025 to integrate the national identification number (NIN) database with enrolled workers’ data held under the ECS. The integration seeks to ease verification of workers’ biodata for compensation, improve the accuracy of employee compensation records, reduce fraudulent claims, and strengthen accountability across Nigeria’s workforce. NIN-based verification will enable the Nigeria Social Insurance Trust Fund (NSITF) to seamlessly authenticate the identities of employees, employers, and claimants under the scheme. To date, NSITF has provided death benefits to more than 39,000 workers and disability and retirement benefits to 50,000 others.

Title: National social safety net program (cash transfers)
KeyTerms: Social protection, Social assistance, Conditional cash transfers
Description: Digital ID verifies beneficiary in Nigeria’s National Social Safety Net Program, coordinated by the National Social Safety-Nets Coordinating Office (NASSCO) under the Ministry of Humanitarian Affairs. The National Social Register (NSR), built in collaboration with the World Bank, serves as Nigeria’s official database of poor and vulnerable households and is being integrated with the national identification number (NIN) to improve data accuracy, eliminate duplication, and ensure cash transfers reach legitimate beneficiaries. As of August 2025, approximately 7.9 million households had been visited and 10.2 million NINs collected, of which NIMC (National Identity Management Commission) successfully validated 9.6 million. By August 2025, at least 8.1 million households had received one or more tranches of NGN 25,000 cash transfers, paid directly into beneficiaries’ bank accounts or mobile wallets following NIN validation. The NIN integration strengthens targeting accuracy, supports financial inclusion of the poorest, enables rapid crisis response, and ensures government resources reach Nigeria’s most vulnerable populations.

Title: National Health Insurance Authority (NHIA) enrollment
KeyTerms: Health insurance, Social protection
Description: Digital ID verifies the identities of citizens during enrollment in health insurance and service access. The National Health Insurance Authority (NHIA) Act, enacted in 2022, mandates that individuals provide a national identification number (NIN) to enroll in any NHIA health insurance plan. New customers must submit their NIN through the NHIA self-service enrollment portal launched in September 2025, which allows them to enroll, manage their details, and access health insurance services online through their NIN, email, or phone number. The mandatory NIN requirement has improved transparency, accountability, and the integrity of enrollment data, reduced fraudulent registrations, and eliminated duplicate accounts. As of November 2025, NHIA coverage reached approximately 21 million Nigerians. The government targets 44 million customers by 2030 as part of Nigeria’s Universal Health Coverage (UHC) strategy. The NIN integration enables accurate beneficiary tracking, supports financial protection by reducing out-of-pocket health expenditure, and facilitates the government’s mandate that all citizens have access to affordable, quality healthcare regardless of socioeconomic status.

Title: SIM registration using NIK number
KeyTerms: SIM registration,  e-KYC
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 1- Single-factor authentication (Demographic data is verified)
Description: In Indonesia, residents submit their Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) and family card (KK) through SMS registration to their telecom operator, which then verifies the data against Dukcapil’s database through an API. The verification returns an instant Yes/No response. If matched, the SIM is activated; if not, the registration is rejected.

Title: Bank Account Opening using NIK and IKD
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer, Customer onboarding, Digital Onboarding, Paperless Banking
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2- Multifactor Authentication (NIK Number and Face Verification)
Description: In Indonesia, residents can open a bank account either in person or remotely through the Identitas Kependudukan Digital (IKD) app. The app facilitates e-KYC through face verification with the resident’s Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) number. Once the resident’s identity is successfully established, the verified details are shared with the bank.

Title: Accessing employment and labour benefits using NIK and IKD
KeyTerms: Social Security, Social Protection
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2- Multifactor Authentication (NIK Number and Face Verification)
Description: In Indonesia, the Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) and Identitas Kependudukan Digital (IKD) app is used to access employment and labor benefits. NIK and IKD are used to verify beneficiaries’ identities during registration and deduplication, and to facilitate claims through Indonesia's Social Security Agency for the Employment Jamsostek Mobile (JMO) app. Beneficiaries complete face authentication, and the system performs 1:1 matching to confirm their identity. Upon successful authentication, beneficiaries can access employment and labor benefits.

Title: Pre-employment cash assistance using NIK
KeyTerms: Social Security, Social Protection, Employability, Upskilling
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2- Multifactor Authentication (NIK Number and Face Verification)
Description: The Indonesian government's pre-employment card program initiative offers cash assistance and online training to job seekers, laid-off workers, and those who seek to enhance their skills. Under this program, Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK)-based verification and facial authentication ensure secure, targeted enrollment and benefits disbursement during registration.

Title: Health App access via NIK
KeyTerms: Health records, Appointments, Vaccination records
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2- Multifactor Authentication (NIK Number and Face Verification)
Description: In Indonesia, residents use their Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) as a unique identifier to log in to the SATUSEHAT app, the official application of the Indonesian Ministry of Health. The system verifies the NIK against the national population database, and users authenticate themselves through face authentication. On successful verification, the app enables residents to access electronic medical records, track vaccinations, monitor health metrics, and manage medication.

Title: Accessing national health insurance via NIK
KeyTerms: Health Insurance, National Health Insurance
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2- Multifactor Authentication (NIK Number and Face Verification)
Description: In Indonesia, residents use their Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) as the primary identifier to access the national health insurance program, Jaminan Kesehatan Nasional (JKN). The NIK confirms identity and eligibility to enroll and access services through the JKN app or at health facilities. Residents authenticate themselves through the JKN app, and upon successful authentication, they can access health insurance services, check membership status, and avail themselves of benefits.

Title: Scholarship application using NIK
KeyTerms: Scholarship, Education
Description: The Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) is required to apply for the LPDP state-funded scholarship. During registration, applicants enter their NIK to verify their identity against the national population database, also known as Dukcapil. This ensures that applicants' demographic details are accurate and prevents duplication or fraud during the selection process.

Title: Conditional Cash Transfer using NIK
KeyTerms: Social Benefit, Financial Aid, Social Security
Description: The Program Keluarga Harapan (PKH), Indonesian for Family Hope Program, is the country's largest conditional cash transfer initiative designed to reduce poverty and improve human capital. It provides quarterly financial assistance to low-income households. The Nomor Induk Kependudukan (NIK) serves as the verification tool for households listed in the central database to determine social aid eligibility under PKH. Upon successful verification, the household is enrolled under PKH, and social assistance is disbursed through bank accounts or e-wallets linked to verified NIKs.

Title: Tax Filing using CPF and gov.br
KeyTerms: Income Tax Return, Tax Filing, Revenue and Taxation
Description: The CPF or individual taxpayer registry serves as the unique taxpayer identifier in Brazil for all income tax–related services. Residents authenticate themselves through gov.br to access the tax portal; once authenticated, they can submit and file their income tax returns.

Title: Direct Benefit Transfer (in kind) using CPF/gov.br
KeyTerms: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), Social Security, Social Protection, Food Security, Food Distribution
Description: The CPF validates the beneficiary's eligibility and cross-checked it against the social registry. Beneficiaries authenticate themselves through gov.br; once authenticated, they can access their benefits.

Title: Direct Benefit Transfer (in cash) using CPF/gov.br
KeyTerms: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), Social Security, Social Protection
Description: Direct benefit transfer (DBT) programs, such as Brazil’s Bolsa Família, are conditional cash transfer (CCT) programs that use the CPF as a unique identifier to cross-check beneficiaries against the unified social registry. Beneficiaries authenticate themselves through gov.br to access the cash transfers.

Title: Registration and Updating Social registry using CPF/gov.br
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Social registry
Description: CPF is the mandatory identifier for individuals to register and update their information in the social registry. The Cadastro Único (CadÚnico), a social registry database, serves as a foundation for other social and labor protection program. Residents authenticate themselves through the gov.br login to register and update their information.

Title: Labour benefit account access using gov.br
KeyTerms: Labour Benefits
Description: The CPF serves as the unique identifier for the Fundo de Garantia do Tempo de Serviço (FGTS), the Portuguese term for the Length-of-Service Guarantee Fund, and other labor benefit accounts. Under the FGTS program, funds are credited to workers’ accounts, and withdrawals are carried out through the FGTS digital platform. Users authenticate themselves through gov.br, with the authentication assurance level based on the account type, namely gold, silver, or bronze.

Title: Accessing vaccination records using gov.br
KeyTerms: Health Records, Vaccination Records, National Digital Health Platform
Description: gov.br is a standard authentication mechanism used to book and access vaccination records. Users can view and download their vaccination certificate by authenticating themselves through gov.br. The authentication factor varies depending on the account type.

Title: Access to the national digital health platform using CPF and gov.br
KeyTerms: Health Records, Vaccination Records, National Digital Health Platform
Description: CPF serves as the primary unique ID to access the national digital health platform. It enables citizens to access their unified health record within the Réseau National des Données de Santé (RNDS), the French National Health Data Network. Citizens authenticate themselves through the gov.br login. The RNDS progressively connects public and private health providers; currently, more than 80% of Brazilian states and 68% of municipalities use RNDS data to organize health services and support public health planning.

Title: Sim registration using CPF and gov.br
KeyTerms: KYC, Electronic Know Your Customers (eKYC), SIM registration, Customer Onboarding
Description: Brazil has more than 260 million active cellular lines, all tied to CPF records. The CPF serves as the unique identifier for SIM card activation. Authentication through gov.br credentials strengthens digital verification for new activations and portability requests. The customer initiates a SIM registration activation request through the telecom service provider's app or website and verifies identity through gov.br. This ties the activation to the correct person and reduces risks, such as identity impersonation and fraudulent activations.

Title: Identity verification in competitive exams using gov.br and CPF/CIN
KeyTerms: Competitive exams, Staff Selection Commission
Description: Candidates must present digital documents for identification exclusively through official applications accessed with their gov.br accounts. The gov.br account serves as the gateway to access digital documents. It supports three security levels, namely bronze, silver, and gold. The higher the level, the more digital services citizens can access. Users can complete verification to upgrade their account level through facial authentication, validation with accredited bank accounts, or proof of identity on official channels.

Title: Higher Education and Scholarship registration using gov.br and CPF/CIN
KeyTerms: Education, Scholarship, Enrollment
Description: Students in Brazil must have a gov.br account to register for higher education admissions and to access the scholarship and student loan portal. They authenticate through the gov.br app or website, and the authentication assurance level varies based on account type, whether gold, silver, or bronze.

Title: Bank account opening using gov.br and CPF/CIN
KeyTerms: Finance, Financial Inclusion, e-KYC, Know-Your-Customer,  Customer onboarding, Digital Onboarding, Paperless Banking
Description: The gov.br platform integrates with accredited banks to authenticate users. Partner banks allow customers to use their banking credentials to log in to gov.br. The gov.br login is also used as a trusted authentication method for onboarding and account opening. A customer uses the bank’s app or website to initiate account opening, authenticates it using gov.br, and then consents to share verified data with banks. By 2024, 14 of the largest banks in Brazil had integrated gov.br login for onboarding.

Title: Digital School Management System via e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: e-School, Education, School Management, Student Records, Digital Education
Description: e-Kool is Estonia’s national digital school management platform that provides a unified digital interface for schools, teachers, students, and parents across the country. User authenticates with their national e-ID, such as mobile-ID or smart-ID, OTP, and PIN1. This ensures secure, verified login for all users. Once authenticated, teachers can record grades, attendance, and homework assignments. Students can view their timetables, grades, and upcoming lessons, while parents can monitor their child’s academic progress, communicate with teachers, and receive notifications from the school.

Title: Land and Property Transaction using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: e-Land Registry, Land Register, Property, Real Estate,  Land Ownership,  Digital Property Records
Description: In Estonia, the e-Land Registry is operated by the Centre of Registers and Information Systems (RIK) under the Ministry of Justice. It provides real-time, transparent access to land and property information with the national digital ID. Citizens, notaries, and legal professionals authenticate using their digital ID credentials, OTP, and PIN1 to access the land and property register. The portal provides updated information about land plots, which include ownership details, mortgages, and boundaries. This makes property due diligence entirely digital and paperless. Property transactions, such as transfers of ownership and creation of mortgages, are processed digitally. Notaries authenticate and sign documents using qualified electronic signatures, PIN2.

Title: Agricultural Registry and Aid via e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: e-PRIA, Agriculture, Agricultural Registry, Agricultural Aid, Farmer Registry, Animal Registry
Description:
In Estonia, e-PRIA is a digital portal for the Agricultural Registers and Information Board (ARIB). It enables farmers, landowners, and agricultural businesses to manage their interactions with national and the EU agricultural support programs entirely online. 
Users authenticate with their national electronic ID credentials, such as mobile-ID and smart-ID; one-time password (OTP); and first personal identification number (PIN1). After successful authentication, they can access the animal registry to manage and update records of livestock and other agricultural animals. They can also apply for national and the EU agricultural aid and subsidies, track the status of their applications, and manage land use data linked to their farm holdings.

Title: Transport e-Services using e-ID (Samrt-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Driving License, Vehicle Registration, Digital Transport Services
Description:
The Transport Authority in Estonia offers a comprehensive suite of digital services accessible through the national electronic ID, such as smart-ID and mobile-ID. This allows residents to manage their transport-related administrative needs entirely online. Users authenticate to the transport administration e-service portal with a one-time password (OTP) and the first personal identification number (PIN1).
On successful authentication, they can check the validity and details of their driving license, submit applications for new driving documents or renewals, and manage vehicle ownership and registration records. They can formalize vehicle ownership digitally and sign the transfer document with PIN2, which eliminates the need for physical presence at transport offices.

Title: Marriage and Divorce Registration using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Civil Registration, Marriage Registration, Divorce Registration, Civil Status, Population Register, Digital Civil Services, Birth Registration, Death Registration
Description: In Estonia, civil registration services are available digitally, which include the registration of births, deaths, marriages, and divorces. Estonia digitalized marriage registration in 2022 and divorce registration in 2024, which completed Estonia's fully digital civil status service portfolio. Citizens authenticate with their national e-ID credentials (mobile-ID or smart-ID). The population register maintain records of civil status events in real-time, with all submissions and approvals digitally signed using a second personal identification number (PIN2).

Title: e-File and Legal Proceedings using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: e-File, Justice System, Court Services, Legal Proceedings,  Digital Court
Description: The e-File system in Estonia, operated by the Ministry of Justice, enables full digital participation in civil, administrative, and criminal court proceedings. All parties to a legal case, residents, lawyers, prosecutors, and court officials, authenticate to the e-File portal through their digital ID credentials through the ID-card or Mobile-ID. On successful authentication, lawyers can electronically submit pleadings, evidence, and other court documents, and parties can monitor the real-time progress of their case and receive notifications of court decisions and deadlines. All submitted documents are digitally signed with a second personal identification number (PIN2), which creates legally binding qualified electronic signatures. The e-File system operates 24/7, which allows legal actions to be filed outside traditional office hours.

Title: Digital Signatures and Document Attestation using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Digital Signature, Qualified Electronic Signature (QES),  Document Signing,  Electronic Signature
Description: In Estonia, citizens, businesses, and government agencies sign documents and contracts digitally with full legal validity. They use their e-ID, mobile-ID, or smart-ID to create a qualified electronic signature (QES). A QES has the same legal value as a handwritten signature. The user enters a one-time password (OTP) and a second personal identification number (PIN2) on their device to sign. This action triggers the secure cryptographic process. The system then adds a timestamp and a certificate chain to the document, which makes it tamper-evident and easy to verify. In Estonia, digital signatures are in use across almost every sector, which includes government approvals, bank authorizations, real estate transactions, healthcare consent, and private contracts, among others.

Title: Labor and Social Benefits using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Social Insurance, Pension, Disability Benefits, Parental Leave, Family Benefits, Social Security
Description: The Estonian Social Insurance Board (ENSIB) provides a wide range of labor and social benefit services accessible through the national State Portal with e-ID.  Residents authenticate themselves with a one-time password (OTP) and their first personal identification number (PIN1) to access their personal social insurance dashboard. Residents can apply for pensions, disability benefits, parental leave, maternity benefits, and other family support programs. They digitally sign applications with PIN2, which creates legally binding qualified electronic signatures.

Title: Unemployment Insurance Services using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Unemployment Benefits, Social Protection, Employment Services, Welfare
Description: The Estonian Unemployment Insurance Fund (Eesti Töötukassa) operates a fully digital service portal that allows Estonian residents to access employment and unemployment services entirely online through their national electronic ID. Citizens authenticate with a one-time password (OTP) and their first personal identification number (PIN1). Once logged in, residents can register as unemployed, browse available job offers that match their profile, apply for unemployment benefits and allowances, manage their benefit claims, and track payment history. The digital ID layer ensures benefit applications are securely linked to verified individual identities. This prevents fraudulent claims and supports precise eligibility assessment.

Title: Bank Account Opening using e-ID (Smart-ID/Mobile-ID)
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, Digital Onboarding, Online Banking, e-KYC
Description: Banks in Estonia allow digital onboarding and day-to-day online banking operations through the national digital ID. Residents authenticate themselves on bank portals and mobile apps using their credentials with a one-time password (OTP) and first personal identification number (PIN1). Upon successful authentication, electronic know your customer (e-KYC) details are shared with banks. For online transactions, fund transfers, and high-value operations, residents authorize these using the PIN2 digital signature, which meets the legal requirements of qualified electronic signatures.

Title: Tax Filing using e-ID (Mobile-ID/Samrt-ID)
KeyTerms: e-Tax, Tax Filing, Income Tax, Tax Return
Description: In Estonia, residents can file their tax return online with their national digital ID. Residents authenticate themselves on the e-tax portal through a one-time password (OTP) and their first personal identification number (PIN1). After successful authentication and login, the portal automatically pre-fills tax return details, such as income and withholding. The system collects this data from employers, banks, and government registries and retrieves it through the X-Road platform. This follows the once-only principle, which ensures residents do not have to submit the same data again. Residents can review the pre-filled information, make any corrections, and submit their declaration by signing with PIN2 to create a qualified electronic signature.

Title: i-Voting (Online Voting) via e-ID (Mobile-ID/Smart-ID)
KeyTerms: i-Voting,  Online Voting, Electoral System, National Electoral Committee
Description: Estonia was the first country in the world to conduct legally binding national elections over the internet, a system introduced in 2005. It allows eligible voters anywhere in the world to cast their ballot securely with their national e-ID credentials (Mobile-ID, or Smart-ID). Voters authenticate themselves through the first personal identification number (PIN1), and the actual vote is digitally signed with PIN2, which generates a qualified electronic signature. The cryptographic process ensures end-to-end integrity and non-repudiation, while anonymity is preserved through vote encryption and the separation of identity from ballot content. The Population Register validates voter eligibility in real time through secure interoperability services.

Title: Health Insurance Fund Access via e-ID (Mobile-ID/Smart-ID
KeyTerms: Health Insurance,  Health Portal,  Digital Health Insurance
Description: The Estonian Health Insurance Fund (EHIF) enables residents to manage their health insurance services digitally through the national health portal. Residents authenticate themselves through a one-time password (OTP) and the first personal identification number (PIN1) to access a personalized health insurance dashboard. On successful authentication, the user can access services once the validity of health insurance coverage is verified. They can also view the entire medical bill history paid by the fund, check reimbursement status for medicines and procedures, and review the electronic health record summary.

Title: e-Prescriptions using e-ID/Mobile-ID/Smart-ID
KeyTerms: e-Health, e-Prescriptions, Healthcare Access, Digital Health
Description: e-Prescription is a nationwide digital prescription system that allows doctors to issue, write, and sign prescriptions electronically with their e-ID. It also enables pharmacies to dispense medicines through the patient’s digital ID. The system stores the prescription in the national e-Health database and links it to the patient’s personal ID number. A resident can collect medicine by showing their e-ID at the pharmacy. The pharmacist accesses the prescription from the central system through the resident’s ID number and dispenses the medicine.

Title: Health Record Access using e-ID/Mobile-ID/Smart-ID
KeyTerms: e-Health, Electronic Health Records, Healthcare Access, Digital Health
Description: In Estonia, residents can securely access and manage their personal health records with the national e-ID, Mobile-ID, or Smart-ID, through the Estonian Health Information System (EHIS). Residents authenticate themselves through a one-time password (OTP) and personal identification number (PIN1). On successful authentication, residents can view their electronic health records, check past consultations, view prescribed medicines, and access vaccination histories.

Title: Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) Issuance using NID Verification
KeyTerms: Taxpayer ID, Tax Registration, Revenue, Digital Services
Description: Bangladesh's National Board of Revenue (NBR) uses the national ID (NID) as the foundational identity credential for issuing taxpayer identification numbers (TINs). All TINs issued in Bangladesh are connected and cross-referenced with the applicant's NID, which creates a unified identity layer between civil registration and tax administration. TIN registration is accessible online and allows citizens to apply by providing their NID, which is verified through a demographic API connected to the Bangladesh Election Commission.

Title: SIM Registration via NID
KeyTerms: SIM Registration, Telecom, e-KYC, Digital Onboarding
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - NID-based demographic verification and biometric
Description: The Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission (BTRC) mandates  biometric verification using national ID (NID) for all SIM card registrations in the country. The registration process requires citizens to present their NID at a telecom operator's outlet or authorized agent outlets, where their fingerprint is captured and matched against the NID biometric database managed by the Bangladesh Election Commission.

Title: Direct Benefit Transfer using NID
KeyTerms: Social Protection, Old Age Allowance, Widow Allowance, Disability Allowance, DBT
Description: Bangladesh uses the national ID (NID) as the identity credential to transfer benefits of its social protection schemes, such as old-age, widow, and disability allowances. NID-based government-to-person (G2P) transfers were introduced during 2018–2019. Beneficiary registration and deduplication are managed through the Single Social Registry. This involves verifying NID credentials through a demographic API call to the Bangladesh Election Commission, supported by OTP authentication.

Title: Agent Banking Account Opening via NID
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, Agent Banking, e-KYC,  Digital Banking, Account Opening
Description: Agent banking operators in Bangladesh use national ID (NID)-based e-KYC for agent bank account opening. Agent banking extends formal financial services to rural and semi-urban areas through authorized agents, and NID-based e-KYC is central to enabling this model at scale. The account opening process involves NID demographic verification through an API with the Bangladesh Election Commission, supplemented by OTP authentication conducted at the agent’s point of service.

Title: Mobile Financial Services (MFS) Account Opening via NID
KeyTerms: Financial Inclusion, MFS, e-KYC,  Account Opening, Digital Onboarding
Description: Bangladesh Bank, in collaboration with mobile financial service (MFS) operators such as bKash and Nagad, uses national ID (NID) and birth registration number (BRN)-based electronic Know Your Customer (e-KYC) for digital account opening. The e-KYC process for MFS account opening involves NID and BRN demographic verification through an API call to the Bangladesh Election Commission, supplemented by an OTP authentication. The NID-based e-KYC system has significantly reduced the time and cost of onboarding new users, which eliminates the need for physical document submission and in-person verification in many cases.

Title: Shared Health Record (SHR) using NID/BRN
KeyTerms: Health, Shared Health Record, Health ID, Health Information Exchange, Digital Health
Description: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare in Bangladesh is developing a Shared Health Record (SHR) system as part of its broader Health Information Exchange (HIE) initiative. The system assigns a unique health ID to each citizen, built on top of the existing national ID (NID) or birth registration number (BRN). The health ID serves as a universal patient identifier that enables authorized healthcare providers to access and share a patient’s clinical history across different facilities. Citizens register using their NID or BRN. Credentials are verified through demographic APIs linked to the Bangladesh Election Commission and the Office of the Registrar General, respectively.

Title: Vaccination Registration using NID/BRN
KeyTerms: Health, COVID-19, Vaccination, NID, Immunisation, Digital Health
Description: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare in Bangladesh deployed the Surokkha app for COVID-19 vaccination registration. Citizens registered using their national ID (NID) or birth registration number (BRN). NID credentials were verified through a demographic API call to the Bangladesh Election Commission, and BRN credentials were verified through a demographic API with the Office of the Registrar General. The Ministry of Health issued OTPs during registration to add a layer of security. Once verified, citizens received vaccination slots at designated centers. Their vaccination status was digitally recorded.

Title: Marine Fisheries Program via NID-Based Fisher ID and DBT
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Fisheries, Fisher ID, Direct Benefit Transfer, G2P
Description: The Sustainable Coastal and Marine Fisheries Program is administered by the Ministry of Fisheries and Livestock. It uses the national ID (NID) as the primary identity credential to register fishermen and deliver cash benefits. Fishermen register using their NID to receive a Fisher Card. The NID is verified through a demographic API linked to the Bangladesh Election Commission, and no OTP or biometric authentication is required. Verified profiles are linked to mobile financial services (MFS) wallets that enable secure and traceable cash transfers to registered fishermen.

Title: Agriculture Subsidies Disbursement via NID
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Subsidy, Direct Benefit Transfer, DBT, Mobile Financial Services, G2P
Description: The Ministry of Agriculture, Bangladesh, uses the national ID (NID) as the primary identity credential to register farmers as subsidy beneficiaries. Farmers register through authorized agents or designated registration points, with NIDs verified through a demographic API linked to the Bangladesh Election Commission. Verified profiles are enrolled in the beneficiary management system and linked to mobile financial services (MFS) wallets, such as bKash or Nagad. This ensures direct, timely, and transparent cash transfer delivery.

Title: Primary Education Stipend using NID/BRN
KeyTerms: Education, Stipend, Scholarship, School Enrollment
Description: The Primary Education Stipend Program (PESP) is one of Bangladesh's oldest and largest digital benefit transfer initiatives in the education sector. The program provides a monthly stipend to mothers of primary school students. Beneficiary enrollment is based on BRN-linked unique IDs (UIDs) assigned to students. Verification of beneficiaries is conducted through the Single Social Registry using the birth registration number (BRN) of the student and the national ID (NID) of their mother. NID verification is performed through a demographic API linked to the Bangladesh Election Commission, while BRN verification is handled through the office of the Registrar General.

Title: Digital voter registration and hybrid election system
KeyTerms: Elections, Civic Participation, Voter Registration, Biometric Verification, Data Exchange, Digital Inclusion, Accessibility, Offline Authentication
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication for election officials and digital systems
Description:
Papua New Guinea's current voter registration and election processes suffer from fragmented identity data, unreliable or outdated voter rolls with duplicate and missing records, and severe access challenges for remote communities. These gaps produce operational and democratic problems including inaccurate electoral rolls that undermine confidence in results, manual paper-based verification and counting processes that are slow, error-prone, and easily disputed, and exclusion of remote populations with poor connectivity. Weak cross-sector identity interoperability between civil registries, telecommunications, and financial institutions prevents fast eKYC and SIM validation needed for trustworthy voter roll preparation. 
This use case proposes a hybrid election system integrating SevisPass Digital ID, SevisDEx (Data Exchange), and National ID (NID) with census data and eKYC processes to create verified voter identity across the entire voting population. The system combines online authentication via SevisDEx with offline Android applications for voter registration in rural areas lacking internet access. 
Traditional ballot counting will eventually transition to digital platforms. This approach reduces duplicate and inflated rolls through authoritative identity linking with biometrics and probabilistic matching, creating single canonical voter records. It improves accessibility by allowing offline-capable wallets and enumerator Android apps to enroll remote citizens without continuous internet. 
It speeds processes and reduces costs through consented, auditable attribute exchange (age, residency) automating eligibility checks and shortening queues. The hybrid model is uniquely suitable for PNG's current economic and social structure, revolutionizing voting systems and eliminating recurring election-period issues across all government agencies.

Title: SADC Digital Farmer ID: Regional Biometric Identity and Resource Access System for Farmers
KeyTerms: Agriculture, Digital Inclusion & Accessibility, Regional Integration, Blockchain, IoT Sensors, Subsidy Management, Food Security
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 2 - Multi-factor authentication at Smart Village Hubs and digital platform access for resource applications
Description:
Southern African farmers lack centralized identity systems and accurate land records, preventing subsidy, loan, and insurance access. Fraudulent claims divert resources; farmers lack climate/price resilience or productivity tools. 
SADC Digital Farmer ID provides revolutionary biometric system empowering farmers through unique digital IDs via fingerprints/facial recognition linked to Farmer Registry. Centralized database contains land size, crops, livestock, water usage, yield history. Features include streamlined subsidy/loan/insurance access, market connections with buyers/cooperatives, blockchain ensuring tamper-proof land/resource records, AI providing personalized advice on planting/pest control/water management, IoT sensors monitoring soil/water usage. 
Smart Village Hubs with biometric scanners provide accessible registration, service access, and training. By 2030, transforms 100 million farmers' lives, enhances food security, ensures sustainable growth, and empowers remote farmers through precision agriculture and market access.

Title: Digital ID-based tax compliance system
KeyTerms: Public Finance, Governance, Tax Administration, Fraud Prevention, Financial Data Integration, Blockchain, AI-Driven Audits
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Multi-factor biometric authentication for tax authority access and financial data verification
Description:
Senegal loses 526.4 billion FCFA annually (3.4% GDP) to tax evasion with 97% of businesses informal and only 6.5% registered under NINEA. Weak enforcement and taxpayer verification enable fraud, limiting infrastructure/social investments. 
QUANTIC integrates digital ID infrastructure with NINEA taxpayer system and financial databases using biometric verification, blockchain for tamper-proof records, and AI-driven fraud detection. Mandatory biometric-verified digital ID for business registration prevents duplicate tax identities and fraudulent shell companies. 
Automated taxpayer verification with real-time business owner verification enables AI-driven audits detecting declaration inconsistencies. Cross-agency data sharing ensures secure interoperability between tax authorities, banks, customs, and business registries. 
Digital payment and electronic invoicing integration ensures transaction traceability. System eliminates fake identities, reduces fraud, enhances government revenue, lowers administrative costs, and brings informal businesses into formal economy promoting financial inclusion and economic growth.

Title: Digital ID-based property ownership and transfer system
KeyTerms: Public Services, Property Management, Blockchain, Smart Contracts, Fraud Prevention, Asset Security, Escrow Management
AuthenticationAssuranceLevel: AAL 3 - Hardware and cryptographic-based authentication for property transfer transactions using blockchain
Description:
